news.summary: A intense battle among lobbyists in Washington is putting at risk one of President Donald Trump's key financial policy goals in Congress.

news.summary: Eleven mid-cap stocks experienced significant growth in the past week, with LZ Technology Holdings Limited (NASDAQ: LZMH ) leading the way with a remarkable 78.57% surge. Centrus Energy Corp. (AMEX: LEU ) also saw an impressive increase of 36.22% following President Donald Trump's signing of executive orders to bolster the U.S. nuclear industry. Meanwhile, e.l.f. Beauty, Inc. (NYSE: ELF ) witnessed a substantial 35.89% rise after surpassing quarterly expectations and unveiling a $1 billion acquisition of Hailey Bieber's Brand Rhode. This move prompted analysts to increase their price target for the stock. For further details, the full story can be found on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Top 11 mid-cap gainers last week include LZ Technology Holdings (+78.57%) and e.l.f Beauty (+35.89%). e.l.f Beauty to acquire Hailey Bieber's brand for $1 billion.

news.summary: President Donald Trump has praised Arthur Laffer's renowned theory, created on a napkin, which suggests that reducing income taxes can potentially increase revenue.
news.headline: Trump praises Laffer's theory that lower taxes raise revenue. Laffer Curve is iconic.

news.summary: Expectations for a market recovery following last year's record low sales figures of the past 29 years have fallen short.
news.headline: Housing market fails to rebound as expected after sales hit 29-year low.

news.summary: Toro Company (NYSE: TTC) announced a 2% year-over-year decrease in second-quarter fiscal 2025 sales, dropping to $1.32 billion which was below the anticipated $1.35 billion. Residential segment sales saw an 11.4% decline year over year, amounting to $297.4 million due to decreased shipments of walk power mowers, zero-turn mowers, and portable power products as well as the previous year's sale of Pope Products. Segment earnings also decreased significantly from $36.1 million to $16.1 million, impacted by increased material, manufacturing, and freight expenses, lower net sales volume, and inventory valuation adjustments. On the other hand, professional segment sales rose by 0.8% year over year to $1.014 billion, propelled by higher deliveries of golf and grounds products, though offset by reduced underground sales.
news.headline: Toro Co. Q2 sales down 2% to $1.32B due to lower residential sales; segment earnings drop to $16.1M; Professional segment sales up 0.8% to $1.014B.

news.summary: Fiscal conservatives are avoiding discussion on a business tax deduction that is widely popular among politicians but has faced scrutiny for not delivering significant economic advantages.
news.headline: Fiscal hawks support tax break despite criticism; business tax deduction remains popular with no end in sight.

news.summary: President Donald Trump is aiming for a robust labor market and decreased interest rates. Currently, achieving both targets simultaneously poses a challenge.
news.headline: Trump wants strong labor market and low rates, but they aren't compatible.

news.summary: Rodney Hood, the Acting Comptroller of the Currency, has expressed readiness for his agency to swiftly take action as Congress nears granting the OCC significant new powers to regulate stablecoins.
news.headline: Senate's megabill will grant OCC new authority to oversee stablecoins. Acting Comptroller Rodney Hood is prepared for action.

news.summary: The U.S. Federal Trade Commission has finished its antitrust examination of Mars Incorporated's planned purchase of Kellanova (NYSE: K). The acquisition was agreed upon in August 2024 for $36 billion, priced at $83.50 per share. Mars, a privately-owned company operating in the pet care, snacking, and food sectors, is eagerly awaiting the final approval from the European Commission to complete the deal. Poul Weihrauch, CEO & Office of the President at Mars, expressed satisfaction with the FTC's review outcomes, as no conditions or remedies were imposed. Of the 28 mandatory regulatory clearances, only the European Commission's approval remains pending. Both Mars and Kellanova anticipate the transaction being finalized by the conclusion of... The complete story can be found on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: FTC approves Mars' $36B deal to acquire Kellanova, waiting for European Commission's decision.145 words.

news.summary: ABM Industries Inc. (NYSE: ABM) saw an increase in shares following the release of their second-quarter FY25 earnings report. The company reported a revenue growth of 4.6% year over year, reaching $2.11 billion, surpassing the expected $2.06 billion. The growth was fueled by a 3.8% organic increase and a 0.8% contribution from acquisitions. Revenue spikes were notably seen in Technical Solutions (+19.3% Y/Y) and Aviation (+9.2% Y/Y), while Business & Industry revenue also rose by 2.6% Y/Y. CEO Scott Salmirs announced that the company secured $1.1 billion in new bookings in the first half of the year, marking an 11% rise from the previous year. Operating profit saw an uptick of 1.2% Y/Y to $82.3 million, while adjusted EBITDA increased to $125.9 million from $121.0 million compared to the same quarter last year. The adjusted EBITDA margin remained steady at 6.2% Y/Y. Adjusted earnings per share stood at 86 cents.
news.headline: ABM Industries beats revenue estimates with $2.11 billion in Q2, driven by 4.6% Y/Y growth, $1.1 billion in new bookings.

news.summary: Rio Tinto Plc (NYSE: RIO) has recently opened its latest iron ore mine, Western Range, a part of a series of replacement projects with a combined annual capacity of around 130 million tonnes. The mine itself is designed to produce up to 25 million tonnes of iron ore each year and could support the present Paraburdoo mining center for up to 20 years. This venture, totaling $2 billion, is a collaboration between Rio Tinto (54%) and Baowu (46%). The completion of the project was on schedule and within the initial budget. All details can be found on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Rio Tinto opens new $2B Pilbara iron ore mine with China's Baowu, producing up to 25 million tonnes/year, expected to sustain for 2 decades.

news.summary: Walgreens Boots Alliance Inc (NASDAQ: WBA) reported third-quarter sales of $38.99 billion, marking a 7.2% increase year over year (or 6.9% on a constant currency basis), surpassing the expected $36.84 billion consensus. This growth was primarily driven by increased sales in the U.S. Retail Pharmacy and International segments. The adjusted operating income for the quarter was $558 million, down from $613 million in the previous year, due to higher incentive accruals, reduced U.S. retail sales, and diminished equity earnings in Cencora, offset by growth in U.S. Healthcare and cost savings within U.S. Retail Pharmacy. The adjusted earnings per share stood at 38 cents, a decrease from 63 cents in the prior year but higher than the anticipated 34 cents, as reported by Benzinga Pro. CEO Tim Wentworth noted that the results for the third quarter showcased ongoing enhancements in the U.S. Healthcare segment and advantages from cost-saving measures.
news.headline: Walgreens beats sales forecast with $38.99 billion in third-quarter sales (+7.2% YoY), but lower operating income. Adjusted EPS at 38 cents, down from 63 cents a year ago. CEO emphasizes U.S. Healthcare segment growth.

news.summary: In a recent update, it has been reported that the unemployment rate has dropped to 4.2%. This decrease represents a significant improvement from the previous rate of 5.0%. Additionally, the economy has added 250,000 new jobs. This positive trend signals a recovery in the job market and provides hope for those seeking employment opportunities. The decrease in the unemployment rate coupled with the increase in new jobs indicates a strengthening economy. Despite ongoing challenges, these numbers point towards a promising outlook for the labor market.
news.headline: Financial regulator embroiled in chaos.

news.summary: President Trump is challenging the resilience of the American economy with a series of rapid-fire tariffs, a significant decrease in immigration rates, and a major tax policy overhaul.
news.headline: Trump's policies are testing economy with tariffs, tax changes, immigration decline causing trouble in Labor data.

news.summary: Tami Zakaria, an analyst at JPMorgan, has increased the price forecast for Oshkosh Corporation (NYSE: OSK) from $92 to $106, with a maintained Neutral rating. During Oshkosh's Investor Day on Thursday, the company revealed its long-term goals until 2028. These targets include projected revenue in the range of $13 billion to $14 billion, adjusted earnings per share between $18.00 and $22.00, and an EBIT margin of 12% to 14%. Zakaria praised the management's growing openness towards...
news.headline: JPMorgan raises Oshkosh price forecast to $106, expects revenue of $13-14 billion, EPS of $18-$22 by 2028.

news.summary: Business leaders are showing more optimism towards President Donald Trump's agenda compared to their response following the "Liberation Day" tariff announcements.
news.headline: Goldman CEO David Solomon is optimistic about President Trump's agenda, contrasting the uncertainty post-tariff announcements.

news.summary: Wall Street rallied on Thursday with the Nasdaq 100 hitting a record high and the S&P 500 nearing its peak. The S&P 500 was up 0.7% at 6,136, just 9 points shy of its all-time high of 6,145. The Nasdaq 100 rose 0.7% to 22,400, marking another intraday record. The Russell 2000 surged 1%. First-quarter GDP was revised downward to -0.5%, attributed to a wider goods trade deficit. Factory orders in May increased by 16.4% to $343.6 billion, the largest jump since July 2014, beating market expectations of an 8.5% gain. Weekly jobless claims fell by 10,000 to 236,000.
news.headline: Stocks surge to new highs on Wall Street as Nasdaq hits record high of 22,400 and S&P 500 approaches all-time high of 6,145. Dollar tumbles to 40-month lows. GDP revised to -0.5%, while factory orders rise by 16.4% in May and jobless claims fall by 10,000.

news.summary: This recent order marks a milestone for Actelis as it signifies the initial implementation of their state-of-the-art GigaLine solutions with a prominent telecom carrier in the US. This showcases the increasing acknowledgment in the market for Actelis' groundbreaking ultra-low-power hybrid-fiber technology, particularly within the MDU sector. The order not only sets the stage for potential extensive growth but also sets the foundation for possible future orders. This order is a significant step for Actelis and highlights the momentum they are gaining within the industry.
news.headline: Actelis lands first order from major US telecom carrier for next-gen Rapid MDU Connectivity Solutions, signaling market recognition with potential for significant expansion.

news.summary: Pfizer Inc. announced the topline results of the Phase 3 BASIS study on Hympavzi (marstacimab) for individuals with hemophilia A or B with inhibitors. The study also supported FDA approval of Hympavzi in October 2024 for patients without inhibitors. The Phase 3 study successfully met the primary endpoint and key secondary bleeding endpoints, showing the superiority of once-weekly subcutaneous Hympavzi in enhancing key bleeding outcomes compared to on-demand treatment. This treatment approach is particularly beneficial in cases where less burdensome options are required. The BASIS trial revealed that prophylactic use of Hympavzi led to a noteworthy reduction in the annualized bleeding rate (ABR). More details can be found on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Pfizer seeks label expansion for hemophilia drug after successful Phase 3 study. FDA approved in 2024, showing significant reduction in bleeding rate.

news.summary: An analyst at JPMorgan, Pinjalim Bora, has reduced the price forecast for Braze, Inc. (NASDAQ: BRZE) from $45 to $47 but maintained an Overweight rating. The company reported quarterly earnings of seven cents per share, surpassing the analyst consensus estimate of five cents per share, along with sales of $162.06 million, exceeding the analyst consensus estimate of $158.66 million. Braze adjusted its FY2026 EPS projection from 31 cents to 35 cents, while increasing the FY2026 sales forecast from $686 million-$691 million to $702 million-$706 million. The analyst highlights that Braze had a strong start to the year. For the complete story, visit Benzinga.com.
news.headline: JPMorgan lowers Braze price forecast to $47, beats Q2 earnings with $162.06M sales. FY2026 EPS guidance revised to 35 cents and sales focus now $702M-$706M.

news.summary: The United States Senate, previously known for its resistance to industry-friendly cryptocurrency legislation, is expected to approve a significant victory for digital asset firms later today. This development marks a significant shift in the Senate's approach to accommodating the needs of the cryptocurrency sector.
news.headline: U.S. Senate set to pass industry-friendly cryptocurrency legislation today, marking a major win for digital asset firms.

news.summary: On June 22, 2025, Diginex Limited (NASDAQ: DGNX), a top provider of Sustainability RegTech solutions, was added to the S&P Global Broad Market Index (BMI), signifying a significant achievement for the Company. This inclusion in the well-known index showcases Diginex's growing presence and impact in the market.
news.headline: Diginex Limited joins S&P Global BMI Index, a milestone achievement in company's growth. Effective June 22, 2025.

news.summary: The Summary of Economic Projections for June will mark the first instance where central bank officials will provide insights on the projected rates, growth, and inflation in light of President Trump's new tariff policies. It will offer a crucial outlook on the economy in the coming months, with key numeric indicators likely to be involved.
news.headline: June report highlights economic projections post-Trump tariff regime, first time policymakers to address rate, growth, inflation.

news.summary: After experiencing market share declines and operational challenges for years, Elanco Animal Health Incorporated (NYSE: ELAN) seems to be making a turnaround. Following investor meetings held by William Blair this week, analysts have upgraded the stock based on a more stable core business and promising developments in its innovation pipeline. Launching new products for both livestock and pet health has positioned Elanco for growth, despite facing tough competition from key players like Zoetis and Merck. In these meetings, discussions on the company's current progress and future plans have provided insights that led to the upgrade. Analyst Brandon Vazquez noted that the core business, accounting for about 85% of sales, has shown signs of stabilization due to enhanced execution efforts after a period of declining market shares. This news was reported on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Elanco Animal Health (NYSE: ELAN) gains investor confidence with stock upgrade due to stabilized core business and promising pipeline efforts, facing competition from Zoetis and Merck.

news.summary: Goldman Sachs predicts that self-driving cars will disrupt the $430 billion U.S. auto insurance market, potentially cutting insurance costs by over half in the next 15 years. Analyst Mark Delaney foresees insurance costs decreasing from $0.50 per mile in 2025 to $0.23 per mile by 2040. This could challenge the current economics of the industry as autonomous vehicles are expected to reduce accidents caused by human error. The U.S. auto insurance market comprises $432 billion, with $360 billion in personal coverage and $72 billion in commercial. As vehicles become safer, risk pricing and legal liability assignment are likely to change drastically, impacting insurers in the long term.
news.headline: Self-driving cars could cut insurance costs by 50% by 2040 as accidents decrease. Current $430B U.S. auto insurance market set to transform.

news.summary: A company is currently integrating artificial intelligence (AI) into its operations with the goal of achieving quicker market penetration and enhancing operational efficiency. The company aims to utilize AI technology to improve its market performance and streamline its business operations. This strategic move is driven by the intent to leverage AI for maximizing market capture while ensuring smoother and more effective functioning. The AI integration is projected to significantly enhance the company's competitive edge and pave the way for increased growth potential.
news.headline: Actelis Networks is implementing AI for faster growth and operational efficiency. Aim to capture markets faster and enhance operations.

news.summary: Amazon's cloud computing division, Amazon Web Services (AWS), recently saw a significant departure with the exit of Vasi Philomin, a key vice president in charge of generative artificial intelligence development and management of the Bedrock service. Philomin, who had been with Amazon for eight years, confirmed his departure in June. He played a crucial role in leading generative AI initiatives and product strategy, overseeing the development of foundational models like Amazon Titan and the creation of Amazon Bedrock. This exit comes at a time when competition for AI talent is heating up. For further details, the full story can be found on a financial news website.
news.headline: Top AI leader Vasi Philomin leaves Amazon after 8 years. He led generative AI and Bedrock service development. Competition for AI talent intensifies.

news.summary: MM recently interviewed Rory Johnston, an oil market analyst at research service Commodity Context, to gain insight into the factors influencing the oil market and potential economic risks. Johnston's expertise in closely monitoring these trends provides valuable information to investors and analysts.
news.headline: Israel-Iran conflict may impact oil market & economy. Rory Johnston, oil market analyst, mentions risks ahead.

news.summary: Today, Wall Street began the week quietly as major indexes remained steady in anticipation of potential trade developments during high-level U.S.-China discussions in London. The S&P 500 stayed flat around 6,000, close to all-time highs following a 25% increase since April 7, while the Nasdaq 100 saw a slight 0.3% climb to 21,830. Small-cap stocks continued their outperformance, with the Russell 2000 rising by 0.7% to reach its highest level since early March. The market lacked significant economic data, resulting in marginal changes for the U.S. dollar and Treasury yields – the 10-year yield hovered at 4.47%, and the 30-year at 4.96%. Gold prices resumed an upward trajectory, increasing by 0.8% to $3,330 per ounce after a two-day dip. Silver, on the other hand, took the spotlight, surging nearly 2% to $36.67 per ounce.
news.headline: Silver reaches 13-year high, Bitcoin surpasses $108,000. S&P 500 flat at 6,000 near record high. Nasdaq 100 up 0.3% to 21,830. Russell 2000 rises 0.7% to highest level since March. Gold at $3,330 per ounce, silver at $36.67. U.S. dollar and Treasury yields stable. Wall Street awaits news from U.S.-China talks in London.

news.summary: President Donald Trump has been criticizing Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell multiple times for his decision not to decrease interest rates.
news.headline: Trump criticizes Powell for not lowering interest rates as the Federal Reserve remains divided.

news.summary: In the latest update, it has been reported that a total of 1,500 new cases of COVID-19 have been confirmed in the past 24 hours. This brings the total number of active cases in the country to 10,000. Additionally, 20 new deaths have been recorded, escalating the total number of fatalities to 500. The government has announced new measures to combat the spread of the virus, including mandatory mask-wearing in public spaces and restrictions on social gatherings.Officials are urging the public to adhere to these guidelines to curb the rising number of infections and prevent further casualties.
news.headline: Horizon Aircraft is now in compliance with Nasdaq Capital Market listing rules.

news.summary: In extended trading on Wednesday, shares of Applied Optoelectronics, Inc. (NASDAQ: AAOI) rose following the announcement of its initial shipment of high-speed data center transceivers to a prominent hyperscale data center client. The company refrained from disclosing the customer's identity but mentioned that the hyperscaler had been "recently re-engaged." This milestone shipment signifies the first volume delivery of such transceivers by the company.
news.headline: Applied Optoelectronics stock rose in after-hours trading after announcing volume shipment of high-speed data center transceivers to a hyperscale customer.
Enerpac Tool Group announces Q3 FY2025 results.

news.summary: Federal regulators have announced a long-awaited proposal to reduce a significant capital requirement for the largest banks. This policy change has been years in the making and aims to ease restrictions on these financial institutions. The new proposal is expected to impact banks with assets totaling in the billions, potentially providing them with more flexibility in their operations.
news.headline: Federal regulators are proposing to scale back a key capital requirement for big banks after many years.

news.summary: Representatives from the top global economies will be meeting on Tuesday to negotiate a potential agreement in order to ease stringent export limitations that have been enforced on one another.
news.headline: US and China engage in trade talks amid rare earths dispute, seeking agreement to ease export restrictions imposed on each other.

news.summary: The President of the European Commission, Ursula von der Leyen, revealed a plan aimed at increasing pressure on Moscow.
news.headline: EU imposes new sanctions on Russia, inc. Nord Stream ban. Ursula von der Leyen seeks to increase pressure on Moscow.

news.summary: After two days of negotiations, leaders announced a framework agreement aimed at formalizing the terms of a truce between superpowers established in Geneva last month.
news.headline: Leaders hail progress in US-China trade talks with a framework agreement after 2 days. Truce terms solidified from Geneva.

news.summary: The recent ruling from the appeals court marked a significant yet temporary win for the Trump administration.
news.headline: U.S. court maintains Trump tariffs during appeal, a key victory for administration. #TradeWar #Tariffs #Trump

news.summary: If the handshake agreement remains intact, it could potentially mitigate a portion of the repercussions stemming from the trade war initiated by President Trump.
news.headline: New China trade agreement may undo damage from trade war - if handshake holds. Deal revisits US-China relations.

news.summary: This action represents one of the initial instances in 2021 where consumer goods have been singled out for increased import duties.
news.headline: Tariffs extended to impact appliances like fridges, dishwashers. Represents one of the initial occasions when consumer goods faced increased import taxes.
Economists foresee inflation rising in response to tariffs, with significant increases expected in the near future.

news.summary: Trump is considering several candidates to replace current Federal Reserve chair Jerome Powell, whose term ends in 2026. Kevin Warsh, a former Federal Reserve governor, is known for tight policy during the Great Recession but has shown openness to looser monetary conditions. Christopher Waller, a sitting Fed governor since 2020, has signaled a possible interest rate cut in July. Scott Bessent, Treasury Secretary under Trump, and Kevin Hassett, former Chair of the Council of Economic Advisers, are also being considered. David Malpass, former World Bank President, has extensive economic experience. The potential changes are influencing bets on rate cuts, impacting the dollar, and raising concerns about the Fed's independence.
news.headline: Trump's Fed Chairman shortlist includes Kevin Warsh, Christopher Waller, Scott Bessent, Kevin Hassett, David Malpass. Fed policy impacting bets on rate cuts and dollar, with potential effects on global markets.

news.summary: Pending home sales in the U.S. increased by 1.8% in May compared to the previous month, and by 1.1% year-over-year, as reported by the National Association of Realtors. All four regions in the U.S. experienced month-over-month increases, with the West region seeing the most significant growth. Year-over-year, contract signings rose in the Midwest and South, but fell in the Northeast and West. Lawrence Yun, the NAR Chief Economist, noted that job gains and rising wages are modestly helping the housing market. There is a housing shortage in the Northeast leading to higher home prices, while the South has more inventory allowing for better negotiation power for buyers. Price declines in the South are viewed as temporary due to strong job creation in the region.
news.headline: NAR reports 1.8% rise in pending home sales in May, 1.1% YoY. All U.S. regions saw increases, except Northeast and West.

news.summary: Officials in the administration have finalized an agreement that will grant the president significant influence over a private company. This deal has the potential to set a precedent for similar agreements in the future.
news.headline: US Steel's 'Golden Share' deal grants Trump remarkable control, setting a template for similar agreements.

news.summary: In the year 2018, the president urged the group to establish closer ties with Russia and abruptly left the summit. Currently, he is aiming to reduce America's military involvement overseas and initiating a larger-scale trade conflict.
news.headline: Trump's return to G7 deepens rift with allies. In 2018, he pushed for Russia's inclusion and left summit abruptly. Now reduces America's military presence and escalates trade war.

news.summary: The central bank is expected to maintain the current interest rates for the fourth consecutive meeting, signaling a potential extension through the summer.
news.headline: Central bank maintains 'wait and see' approach amidst new risks, keeping interest rates unchanged for the fourth consecutive meeting. Possible extension through summer.

news.summary: A recent study by BCC Research forecasts the "Cold Chain Regional Analysis Market: Middle East and North Africa" to grow to $41.1 billion by 2030, with a projected CAGR of 8.8% from 2025-2030. The report evaluates market dynamics, technological advancements, and regulatory factors in the MENA cold chain industry, focusing on food, pharmaceutical, and chemical sectors. It segments the market by temperature range, technology, applications, and services, excluding Turkey and Israel due to their advanced infrastructure. With a surge in temperature-controlled logistics in MENA driven by food, pharmaceutical, and e-commerce industries, the demand for advanced cold chain solutions is rising due to tightening regulations on food safety and pharmaceutical storage. Significant growth factors include the rise in perishable food and pharmaceutical demand, expansion of e-commerce, government regulations, investments in cold storage infrastructure and smart technologies, and the MENA region's strategic location for trade activities. Saudi Arabia and the UAE are notable investors in cold storage as part of their food security strategies. Advanced technologies like IoT, blockchain, and AI are enhancing cold chain efficiency and compliance. This growth presents strategic opportunities for businesses looking to expand in the evolving MENA logistics market.
news.headline: MENA Cold Chain Market to reach $41.1B by 2030 with 8.8% CAGR. Rising demand for fresh food and pharmaceuticals driving growth.

news.summary: Lawmakers from the party have suggested alterations to the tax code that may provide significant advantages to businesses.
news.headline: GOP senators suggest modifications to House bill, emphasizing pro-business tax code changes.

news.summary: Money Management International (MMI) has introduced a new Debt Resolution Plan (DRP) to assist Americans struggling with debt. The national nonprofit aims to help clients who are falling behind on payments to creditors not participating in a traditional Debt Management Plan (DMP). FDIC filings indicate a rise in charge-offs by lenders due to growing debt balances, delinquencies, and interest rates. MMI's CEO, Jim Triggs, noted the rise in demand for help and the necessity for alternative solutions. The DRP serves individuals with debts already charged off or in collections, expanding MMI's services to a wider financial crisis-stricken population. This move comes amidst increasing financial challenges faced by many Americans.
news.headline: MMI unveils Debt Resolution Plan to assist those struggling with debt. Targets clients with non-participating creditors.CEO cites growing demand for new solution amid surging debt, delinquencies. Full story on Benzinga.com.
